corrected_fields.summary: The FOMC voted 11-1 to maintain the federal funds rate target range at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent. The statement noted solid economic expansion, low job gains, and somewhat elevated inflation. Stephen I. Miran dissented, preferring a 25bps rate cut. The Committee emphasized commitment to the 2% inflation target while monitoring economic risks including Middle East developments.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: The unanimous decision to hold rates signals the Fed remains cautious despite solid growth. The lone dissent from Miran suggests some members see room for easing. The unchanged stance with emphasis on data dependency keeps markets attentive to incoming economic releases for future rate guidance.
corrected_fields.macro_context: Fed maintains rates at 3.5-3.75% with 11-1 vote, citing solid growth and stable employment while monitoring Middle East uncertainty and inflation risks above 2% target.
